$comment: Worked example: 7-Zip 24.09 (x64) MSI for Intune Win32 deployment. Both Build-Win32Package.ps1 and Upload-Win32App.ps1 read this file. Update vendorUrl + sha256 + version when a new release ships, then re-run both scripts.

intuneApp.description: 7-Zip file archiver. Deployed for KEV remediation via Sentinel automation.

supersedence.comment: Optional: list previous app GUIDs in Intune that this version supersedes. Configured via separate Graph PATCH after upload because Win32 LOB supersedence is its own subresource.

schemaNotes.vendorUrl: Direct download URL for the canonical vendor installer. HTTPS required.
schemaNotes.sha256: SHA256 hash from the vendor's published checksum. Build script aborts if mismatch.
schemaNotes.intuneApp.notes: Free-text Intune Notes field. Convention: 'KEV-mapping: <vendor>/<software>' so a human can audit which Intune app maps to which MDETVM_CL row.
schemaNotes.msiInformation.productCode: MUST match the MSI's actual ProductCode. msiexec /x will silently no-op if wrong. Check with: msiexec /a <msi> TARGETDIR=C:\extracted then look at Product.wxs.
schemaNotes.detectionRules: Use 'msi' type for any MSI; Intune compares ProductCode + ProductVersion against installed software. For non-MSI EXE installers use 'registry' or 'file' type.
